authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR Part 1005, Electronic Fund Transfers (Regulation E)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. rule set governing electronic fund transfer error resolution, unauthorized-transfer handling, investigation timing, and prepaid-account obligations relevant to fintech fraud operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Unauthorized-transfer intake, investigation timing, and completion gating

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers FAQs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guidance clarifying how the EFT framework applies in operational scenarios involving unauthorized transfers, digital wallets, and prepaid products common in fintech and embedded finance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Case interpretation and evidence handling for unauthorized-transfer review
used_for: boundary handling when product details change obligations

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) filing obligations and fraud-activity escalation guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary AML authority source defining when suspicious-activity review must escalate rather than remain inside routine fraud-operations handling.

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Issues Analysis of Identity-Related Suspicious Activity
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN analysis of identity-related suspicious-activity patterns that materially differentiates fintech fraud operations from generic customer support or generic fraud review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Identity-related fraud pattern recognition and escalation
used_for: synthetic identity and mule pattern handling

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63A-4, Identity Proofing and Enrollment Guidelines
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public workflow reference for identity-proofing evidence and enrollment controls used in fraud operations handling of identity and account-opening abuse.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Identity-evidence validation during fraud review and rework
used_for: adjacent-lane routing to KYC or identity specialists

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63B-4, Authentication and Authenticator Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public workflow reference for authenticator events, session risk, and step-up verification evidence used in account-takeover fraud operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Session, authentication, and account-takeover evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS v4.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-card control standard for access, logging, and evidence handling that constrains how card-fraud operations may collect, retain, and reference sensitive artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence retention, logging, and sensitive-data handling

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate case ownership, product and payment-rail context, transaction truth, customer notice, and evidence sufficiency before any disposition recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Transaction and case evidence normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize ledger, processor, dispute, identity, session, and communication artifacts so the fraud case has a consistent evidence bundle and lifecycle state.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Fraud review, lifecycle progression, and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Move work through queued, under-review, blocked, escalated, rework, ready-for-disposition, and completed states based on transaction evidence and tenant controls.

workflow_stages.name: Consumer-impact and suspicious-activity escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when unauthorized-transfer obligations, suspicious movement of funds, identity abuse, AML indicators, or customer harm concerns change whether the case may safely proceed.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce case-disposition and completion records only when action history, customer-impact handling, and required approval or communication evidence are present.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Fraud case packet with claim reason, transaction identifiers, account context, and payment-rail classification
Core ledger, card processor, ACH, wallet, prepaid, or program-manager transaction records
Customer-notice logs, communication artifacts, and dispute or servicing notes
Device telemetry, session risk indicators, authenticator events, and identity-verification results
KYC, AML, sanctions, suspicious-activity, and escalation records
Refund, reversal, reimbursement, freeze, hold, and approval-history artifacts
Audit logs and completion evidence records tied to case state changes
systems_of_record: Core banking, wallet, or ledger platforms
Card processor, ACH processor, or payments gateway systems
Identity, KYC, and authentication telemetry systems
AML, sanctions, and compliance monitoring systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on fintech fraud-operations ownership.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe case progression.
Prepare execution status updates, exception notes, servicing summaries, and completion evidence records tied to named artifacts.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
escalate: Missing or contradictory transaction, processor, customer-notice, or tenant-policy evidence materially changes the outcome.
AML, SAR, sanctions, legal, reimbursement, or customer-liability interpretation is required.
Identity, ATO, mule, or suspicious funds-movement indicators change whether the case may safely proceed.
Sponsor-bank, processor, or tenant policy conflicts affect the case disposition or customer-impact treatment.
refuse: Requests to fabricate approvals, regulated advice, reimbursement decisions, or completion evidence.
Requests that require legal, AML, policy-override, reimbursement, or account-action approval authority outside the lane.
Requests to mark work complete while disposition evidence, required communication, or approval evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a fraud case without ledger, processor, or customer-notice truth sufficient to explain the current state.
Treating generic support notes as adequate evidence for a fintech fraud decision or completion record.
Allowing reimbursement, freeze, or liability decisions to appear implied when approval authority is missing.
Ignoring suspicious identity, mule, or account-takeover indicators embedded in transaction or authentication data.
Closing a case without traceable action history, customer-impact handling, or approved closure evidence.
Applying public baseline guidance when tenant sponsor-bank or processor controls require stricter holds, approvals, or outreach.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific definition of when sponsor-bank approval is required before reimbursement or account restriction recommendations can be packaged
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant approval thresholds and owner maps before final action packaging and hold completion until the designated owner is identified.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a tenant treats processor events or internal ledger events as the controlling record of truth for certain embedded-finance programs
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant system-of-record precedence rules and block high-impact state changes until the designated record source is confirmed.
